attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CVD (Cardiovascular) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CVD casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SEQ SPEC-CARD-01-00231 derived from source id CDV-031 — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-CARD-01 bucket at ingest --reseq.
Patient age not stated in source ('elderly'); inferred age 78 / age_band older_adult from 'frail appearing', BPH on tamsulosin, and fall context — please confirm on clinician review.
Sex inferred male from benign prostatic hyperplasia / tamsulosin therapy.
Geographic context not inferable from source — demographics.geographic_context omitted.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person after-hours assessment. Clinician-measured active-stand orthostatic vitals, cardiac auscultation and neurological examination were sealed into 10/11 (physical_sign / requires_specific_investigation) with telehealth_limits; only patient-reportable resting pulse and weight placed in 01.objective_data_offered.

Baseline tier set to T3 (urgent same-day in-person assessment), NOT T5: patient recovered and haemodynamically stable, but orthostatic vitals + ECG to exclude a cardiac cause cannot be obtained via telehealth and fall risk is high (lives alone). This is a deliberate honest-tier discrimination, not a downgrade of the source's acuity.

No drug dose synthesised: source did not state the tamsulosin dose; management is to WITHHOLD pending GP review (deprescribing_note), so no dose_route_frequency was authored. No paediatric dosing involved (adult).
AMT/PBS item codes for tamsulosin omitted rather than guessed — flagged for terminology-receipt assignment at ingest.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
